fallacy.explanation_en: a mistaken belief, especially one on which a theory or argument is based.
fallacy.example_sentence: It's a common fallacy that you need to be born with talent to be creative.
fallacy.original_sentence: First, let's clear up a common fallacy about the meaning of creative thinking.

creative thinking.explanation_en: the ability to use imagination to produce new ideas or things.
creative thinking.example_sentence: Creative thinking is important for solving problems.
creative thinking.original_sentence: First, let's clear up a common fallacy about the meaning of creative thinking.

tabbed as.example_sentence: These books were tabbed as the best for beginners.
tabbed as.original_sentence: For some illogical reason, science, engineering, art, and writing got tabbed as about the only truly creative pursuits.

pursuits.explanation_en: activities that someone does regularly, especially as a hobby or for enjoyment.
pursuits.original_sentence: For some illogical reason, science, engineering, art, and writing got tabbed as about the only truly creative pursuits.

associate with.explanation_en: to connect someone or something in your mind with someone or something else.
associate with.example_sentence: Many people associate this song with their childhood.
associate with.original_sentence: Most people associate creative thinking with things like the discovery of electricity, or a polio vaccine,

discovery.explanation_en: the act of finding something that was not known before.
discovery.example_sentence: The discovery of penicillin changed medicine forever.
discovery.original_sentence: Most people associate creative thinking with things like the discovery of electricity, or a polio vaccine,

development.explanation_en: the process of developing something or of being developed.
development.example_sentence: The development of new technologies is rapid.
development.original_sentence: or the writing of a novel, or the development of color television.

accomplishments.explanation_en: things that have been done successfully, especially by working hard.
accomplishments.example_sentence: Her accomplishments in science are remarkable.
accomplishments.original_sentence: Certainly, accomplishments like these are evidence of creative thinking.

evidence of.explanation_en: proof that something exists or is true.
evidence of.example_sentence: His smile was evidence of his happiness.
evidence of.original_sentence: Certainly, accomplishments like these are evidence of creative thinking.

forward step.explanation_en: a movement or progress towards a better or more advanced state.
forward step.example_sentence: This new policy is a significant forward step for the company.
forward step.original_sentence: Each forward step made in the conquest of space is the result of creative thinking, lots of it.

conquest.explanation_en: the act of defeating a country or group of people by invading it.
conquest.example_sentence: The conquest of new territories was common in ancient history.
conquest.original_sentence: Each forward step made in the conquest of space is the result of creative thinking, lots of it.

reserved for.explanation_en: kept or set aside for a particular person or purpose.
reserved for.example_sentence: This parking spot is reserved for disabled drivers.
reserved for.original_sentence: But creative thinking is not reserved for certain occupations, nor is it restricted to super-intelligent people.

restricted to.explanation_en: limited to a particular group or area.
restricted to.example_sentence: Access to the club is restricted to members only.
restricted to.original_sentence: But creative thinking is not reserved for certain occupations, nor is it restricted to super-intelligent people.

super-intelligent.explanation_en: having or showing very high intelligence.
super-intelligent.example_sentence: The story featured a super-intelligent robot.
super-intelligent.original_sentence: But creative thinking is not reserved for certain occupations, nor is it restricted to super-intelligent people.

devises.explanation_en: plans or invents something in a clever or complicated way.
devises.example_sentence: She devised a clever plan to save money.
devises.original_sentence: A low-income family devises a plan to send their son to a leading university.

leading university.explanation_en: a university that is considered to be among the best and most respected.
leading university.example_sentence: He hopes to study at a leading university after high school.
leading university.original_sentence: A low-income family devises a plan to send their son to a leading university.

undesirable.example_sentence: They found the noise from the construction site undesirable.
undesirable.original_sentence: A family turns the street's most undesirable lot into the neighborhood beauty spot.

neighborhood.explanation_en: an area of a town or city where people live.
neighborhood.example_sentence: We have a friendly neighborhood watch program.
neighborhood.original_sentence: A family turns the street's most undesirable lot into the neighborhood beauty spot.

beauty spot.explanation_en: a place that is very attractive or beautiful.
beauty spot.example_sentence: The park has become a popular beauty spot for tourists.
beauty spot.original_sentence: A family turns the street's most undesirable lot into the neighborhood beauty spot.

minister.explanation_en: a member of the clergy in some Christian churches.
minister.example_sentence: The minister gave a sermon on forgiveness.
minister.original_sentence: A minister develops a plan that doubles his Sunday evening attendance.

attendance.explanation_en: the act of going to or being present at a place, event, or meeting.
attendance.example_sentence: The attendance at the lecture was very high.
attendance.original_sentence: A minister develops a plan that doubles his Sunday evening attendance.

figuring out.explanation_en: to discover the solution to a problem or the answer to a question.
figuring out.example_sentence: He's still figuring out how to fix the car.
figuring out.original_sentence: Figuring out ways to simplify record-keeping, selling the impossible customer, keeping the children occupied constructively,

simplify.explanation_en: to make something easier to understand or do.
simplify.example_sentence: We need to simplify the process.
simplify.original_sentence: Figuring out ways to simplify record-keeping, selling the impossible customer, keeping the children occupied constructively,

record-keeping.explanation_en: the activity of keeping detailed written records of something.
record-keeping.example_sentence: Good record-keeping is essential for any business.
record-keeping.original_sentence: Figuring out ways to simplify record-keeping, selling the impossible customer, keeping the children occupied constructively,

selling the impossible customer.explanation_en: successfully persuading a customer who is very difficult or seems unlikely to buy.
selling the impossible customer.example_sentence: The salesperson was skilled at selling the impossible customer.
selling the impossible customer.original_sentence: Figuring out ways to simplify record-keeping, selling the impossible customer, keeping the children occupied constructively,

occupied constructively.explanation_en: kept busy in a useful or productive way.
occupied constructively.explanation_zh: 有建设性地占用（时间、精力）
occupied constructively.example_sentence: The children were occupied constructively with building blocks.
occupied constructively.original_sentence: Figuring out ways to simplify record-keeping, selling the impossible customer, keeping the children occupied constructively,

making employees really like their work.explanation_en: creating a situation where employees feel genuine enjoyment and satisfaction in their jobs.
making employees really like their work.explanation_zh: 让员工真正喜欢自己的工作
making employees really like their work.example_sentence: A good manager focuses on making employees really like their work.
making employees really like their work.original_sentence: making employees really like their work, or preventing a certain quarrel.

preventing.explanation_en: stopping something from happening or from being something.
preventing.example_sentence: Regular exercise is good for preventing illness.
preventing.original_sentence: making employees really like their work, or preventing a certain quarrel.

quarrel.example_sentence: They had a quarrel over who should do the dishes.
quarrel.original_sentence: making employees really like their work, or preventing a certain quarrel.

practical.explanation_en: concerned with actual use or practice rather than theory.
practical.example_sentence: This is a practical solution to the problem.
practical.original_sentence: All of these are examples of practical, everyday creative thinking.

everyday.explanation_en: ordinary or common; happening or used every day.
everyday.example_sentence: She enjoys the simple pleasures of everyday life.
everyday.original_sentence: All of these are examples of practical, everyday creative thinking.

finding new improved ways.explanation_en: discovering methods that are better or more effective than before.
finding new improved ways.example_sentence: The company is always finding new improved ways to serve its customers.
finding new improved ways.original_sentence: Creative thinking is simply finding new improved ways to do anything.

rewards.explanation_en: things that you get because you have done something good or useful.
rewards.example_sentence: Hard work often brings its own rewards.
rewards.original_sentence: The rewards of all types of success, success in the home, at work,

hinge on.example_sentence: The success of the project will hinge on the team's cooperation.
hinge on.original_sentence: The rewards of all types of success, success in the home, at work, in the community, hinge on finding ways to do things better.

develop and strengthen.explanation_en: to make something grow and become more powerful or effective.
develop and strengthen.example_sentence: It's important to develop and strengthen your skills.
develop and strengthen.original_sentence: Now, let's see what we can do to develop and strengthen our creative thinking ability.

ability.explanation_en: the power or skill to do something.
ability.example_sentence: She has a natural ability for music.
ability.original_sentence: Now, let's see what we can do to develop and strengthen our creative thinking ability.
